domain: Dual Coding Theory & Color-Based Visual Encoding
purpose: Justify color-matched UI in Learning Looper game — airspace class buttons match sectional chart depiction

sources.title: The Influence of Colour on Memory Performance: A Review
Color Education: Methods of Influence on Memory
Chromatic Mnemonics: Boosting Memory with Color Psychology
Dual Coding: A Teacher's Guide to Visual Learning

key_findings.dual_coding_theory: Paivio (1960s): brain processes verbal and visual information through separate channels. Information encoded in BOTH channels is significantly easier to recall than single-channel encoding.
key_findings.color_ranking: Color-coded text segments ranked #1 out of 7 techniques for activating memory retention (ScienceDirect).
key_findings.contrast_effect: Higher color contrast = more attention = better visibility and encoding of information.
key_findings.free_reinforcement: When UI elements are color-matched to real-world representations (e.g., blue for Class B, magenta for Class C), learners encode the visual association passively during every interaction — even when the question topic is unrelated to chart depiction.

A.reason: Not depicted on charts — neutral gray

B.reason: Solid blue — matches sectional chart solid blue lines

C.reason: Magenta — matches sectional chart solid magenta lines

D.reason: Blue dashed — lighter blue with dashed border to match dashed blue chart lines

E.reason: Faded magenta — matches magenta vignette shading on charts

G.reason: Near-white / no color — matches absence of depiction on charts
